overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted professional presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated behavior. With a combined following of 3,580, his posts consistently yield high engagement from academic and data science communities, particularly when sharing research breakthroughs, hiring opportunities, and software tool releases.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, papers, and conference presentations focused on detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Hiring announcements for postdocs and recruitment of PhD students in computational social science.
Updates on software development (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions regarding cross-platform analysis.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active sharing culture by inviting discussion and thread interactions on newly published papers.
Utilize visual elements, such as infographic summaries or team photos, on LinkedIn to further boost click-through and interaction rates on hiring and grant posts.
Maintain a regular cross-platform scheduling strategy, prioritizing technical tool details on Mastodon and high-level milestones on LinkedIn.

platformInsights.bluesky: Acts as the most active hub for peer-to-peer amplification and community growth, yielding the highest average engagement rate, driven heavily by sharing research opportunities and publications.
platformInsights.mastodon: Attracts a highly technical and methodology-focused audience, showing a strong interest in software development releases and analytical tool updates.
platformInsights.linkedin: Serves as the primary channel for broad professional reach, yielding the highest absolute volume of impressions, likes, and comments, making it highly effective for recruitment and funding milestones.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
